subject: Public Health
title: Germ Theory Changed Everything
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Germ Theory Changed Everything.

hook.text: For most of history, disease was blamed on bad air, curses, or divine punishment.

buildup.text: Louis Pasteur and Robert Koch proved that microorganisms cause specific diseases in the 1860s-80s.

discovery.text: Germ theory led to antiseptic surgery, pasteurization, and sterilization—saving countless lives.

twist.text: Ignaz Semmelweis proved handwashing prevented death in the 1840s—but doctors ridiculed and fired him.

climax.text: It took 20 years for the medical establishment to accept what Semmelweis proved with data.

punchline.text: The right answer isn't enough. You need people to believe it.

quiz.question: What happened to Semmelweis when he promoted handwashing?
options: He was ridiculed and fired by the medical establishment
